name: Planet X Nibiru Full
description: The Planet X/Nibiru theory posits the existence of a hidden planet, often referred to as Nibiru, which periodically approaches Earth, causing catastrophic events. Originating from interpretations of ancient Sumerian texts by Zecharia Sitchin, the theory suggests Nibiru is inhabited by an advanced alien race, the Anunnaki, who influenced early human civilization. Proponents cite perceived astronomical anomalies, ancient prophecies, and interpretations of Sumerian mythology as evidence. They often point to increased earthquake activity, volcanic eruptions, and extreme weather events as signs of Nibiru's impending arrival. Mainstream science dismisses the Planet X/Nibiru theory as pseudoscience. Astronomers have extensively searched the solar system and found no evidence of a large planet on such an orbit. They attribute astronomical anomalies to known gravitational interactions and observational errors. The interpretation of Sumerian texts is also heavily contested by Assyriologists, who argue that Sitchin's translations are inaccurate and misrepresent Sumerian cosmology. The persistent nature of the theory is often attributed to fear of global catastrophes and a distrust of established scientific institutions.
subtitle: Catastrophic Planet X collision theory

claims.claim: Nibiru exists and follows a highly elliptical orbit, returning to the inner solar system every 3,600 years.
claims.evidence: Zecharia Sitchin's interpretation of Sumerian texts and cylinder seals, perceived astronomical anomalies.
claims.criticism: Astronomers have found no evidence of such a planet. Sitchin's translations are disputed by Assyriologists.

claims.claim: Nibiru's passage causes catastrophic events on Earth, including earthquakes, tsunamis, and pole shifts.
claims.evidence: Increased seismic activity, volcanic eruptions, extreme weather events interpreted as signs of Nibiru's approach.
claims.criticism: These events are explained by natural geological and meteorological processes. There is no scientific evidence linking them to a hypothetical planet.

claims.claim: The Anunnaki, inhabitants of Nibiru, visited Earth in the past and influenced human civilization.
claims.evidence: Sitchin's interpretation of Sumerian mythology, ancient structures and artifacts.
claims.criticism: Mainstream archaeology and anthropology offer alternative explanations for the origin of human civilization and the construction of ancient structures.

keyFigures.name: Zecharia Sitchin
keyFigures.role: Author who popularized the Nibiru theory.

keyFigures.name: Michael E. Brown
keyFigures.role: Astronomer who discovered dwarf planet Eris, demonstrating the complexities of outer solar system objects and indirectly debunking simple Planet X notions.

timeline.event: Zecharia Sitchin publishes 'The 12th Planet', introducing the Nibiru theory.
The Nibiru cataclysm gains popularity online, often linked to specific dates of predicted planetary alignment or collision.
The Nibiru theory continues to circulate, often adapting to new astronomical discoveries and societal anxieties.

sources.author: Michael Heiser
sources.work: The Myth of a Sumerian Planet X
sources.title: Michael Heiser

sources.author: C. Leroy Ellenberger
sources.work: Web of Lies: A Skeptical Look at ZetaTalk
sources.title: C. Leroy Ellenberger
